title: Humboltia semipellucida
taxonomy: Humboltia semipellucida belongs to the family Apocynaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family. The genus Humboltia is relatively small and consists of a few species native to Central and South America. As part of the Apocynaceae family, Humboltia semipellucida is closely related to other plants such as Vinca and Mandevilla. Its classification is an important aspect of understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
